prompt_summary: Create a messy, production-real ashby-style job application (single-page, large, tier=high). Widgets: combobox. Required: Inputs laid out in table cells, not flex card stacks only Required: Label text split across multiple spans with no space between words Required: Apply form hosted inside iframe or srcdoc iframe shell Required: Yes/no question as paired button elements, not native radio inputs Required: Fields inside a shadow DOM host or declarative shadow root Reference DOM (Readonly prefilled email): Email input is readonly with prefilled value from profile import Reference DOM (Role group not fieldset): Grouped radios use div role=group instead of fieldset Reference DOM (Drag drop file upload): Hidden file input inside drag-and-drop zone with custom button Do NOT produce a tutorial-style form where every field is label[for]+native input. Mix native controls with non-semantic production DOM from the patterns below. Implement ALL required complexity patterns: table-layout, glued-label-text, iframe-apply, pill-yes-no, shadow-host. Include at least one custom widget (div role=textbox, pill buttons, or listbox combobox). Include at least one labeling strategy besides label-for (aria-label, aria-labelledby, glued spans, placeholder-only). Layout: nested sections with h3 headings instead of labels. Avoid generic centered SaaS card as the only structure. High tier: include shadow DOM host OR iframe apply shell OR wizard/conditional reveal with hidden fields. Mirror structural messiness like these references: Readonly prefilled email; Role group not fieldset; Drag drop file upload.
